reasoning:
The question asks whether there will be >10 times the baseline fatalities in Mongolia in the 30 days preceding two resolution dates. The baseline from Jan 8, 2026, was 1.000, signifying exactly 0 ACLED-recorded fatalities over the preceding 360 days. To trigger a "YES", 11 or more fatalities would need to occur within a 30-day window. Our web searches show that Mongolia is a peaceful country with very little fatal political violence or terrorism — the most notable mass casualty event of violence found was a riot with 5 deaths back in 2008. While there has been some political instability in 2025, there have been no armed conflict, terror attacks, or violent riots reported in late 2025 or early 2026. 

Since there is no evidence of recent fatalities between late December 2025 and mid-January 2026, the probability for the first resolution date (Jan 25, 2026) is extremely close to 0. The probability for the second resolution date (Feb 17, 2026) is also similarly extremely low, simply representing the risk of an unforeseen "black swan" event like a terrorist attack. Therefore, we use the absolute minimum probabilities permitted by the system limits, incrementally increasing for the farther horizon to reflect slightly increased uncertainty: [0.05, 0.06].
